title: T-003 — maintenance_schedules + mwo_checklist_templates DDL + RLS (13-b, MNT §9.4)
prompt:
# T-003 — maintenance_schedules schema (PM engine input)

PRD: docs/prd/13-MAINTENANCE-PRD.md §9.4, §8.1 (`pm_schedule_due_engine_v1`), §11.7 (V-MNT-23 operation_context)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create `maintenance_schedules` table (PM/calibration/sanitation/inspection schedules) + a small `mwo_checklist_templates` ref table referenced by FK. Calendar OR usage-counter intervals supported; usage-based marked P2-allowed but column present.

## Pre-flight check
Verify (a) latest migration number in `packages/db/migrations/`, (b) `equipment` table exists (T-002), (c) `technician_profiles` exists (T-002), (d) `app.current_org_id()` function present.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_b_schedules.sql` with CREATE TABLE per PRD §9.4 DDL: `interval_basis CHECK IN ('calendar_days','usage_hours','usage_cycles')`, `schedule_type CHECK IN ('preventive','calibration','sanitation','inspection')`, `operation_context JSONB` (V-MNT-23) GIN-indexed, `next_due_date DATE`, `warning_days INT DEFAULT 7`, FK to `equipment(id)` and `technician_profiles(id)`.
2. `mwo_checklist_templates` table for `maintenance_schedules.checklist_template_id` FK with `org_id`/`site_id`/RLS.
3. R13 audit cols + ENABLE+FORCE RLS + policy referencing `app.current_org_id()` + `site_user_access`.
4. Index `idx_schedules_next_due ON maintenance_schedules(next_due_date) WHERE active`.
5. Drizzle schema in `packages/db/src/schema/maintenance/schedules.ts`.
6. Vitest covering: schedule_type/interval_basis CHECK enforcement, RLS isolation, idx_schedules_next_due used by `EXPLAIN` of canonical PM-engine query.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_b_schedules.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/maintenance/schedules.ts [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/index.ts [modify]
- packages/db/__tests__/maintenance-b-schedules.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration runs, when `\d maintenance_schedules` is read, then schedule_type/interval_basis CHECK constraints + GIN index on operation_context + RLS+FORCE RLS are present.
2. Given two orgs and `app.set_org_context`, when SELECT runs in each session, then RLS isolation holds and policy text references `app.current_org_id()`.
3. Given a row with `interval_basis='usage_hours'`, when inserted, then it is accepted (column present for P2; usage-counter consumption engine deferred but schema supports it).
4. Given `EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM maintenance_schedules WHERE next_due_date <= today AND active`, when run, then the planner uses `idx_schedules_next_due`.

## Test strategy
- RED: maintenance-b-schedules.test.ts asserting four ACs.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test maintenance-b-schedules`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not name business column tenant_id; use org_id.
- Do not read current_setting() GUC; use app.current_org_id().
- Do not omit GIN index on operation_context (V-MNT-23 query speed).
- Do not couple PM engine cron to this task — T-009 covers engine implementation.

pipeline_inputs.description: Create maintenance_schedules + mwo_checklist_templates with RLS, calendar+usage interval enum, operation_context JSONB GIN index, R13 audit, FK to equipment/technicians.
pipeline_inputs.details: PRD §9.4 verbatim DDL with org_id/site_id REC-L1. operation_context JSONB supports V-MNT-23 cross-ref to 02-SETTINGS manufacturing_operations §8.9 — soft reference (no FK; validated by Server Action against ref table). usage_hours/usage_cycles columns present but PM engine T-009 only honors calendar_days in P1.
